title: ANIMA Stage 2: Gradient w_lr adaptation fails (signal too weak)

content.purpose: Test whether gradient-based w_lr adaptation (err EMA driving gradual w_lr push) beats fixed w_lr.

methodology.approach: Gradient-based: err_ema tracks exponential moving average of |err|. Push signal nudges w_lr toward lower values when err is high.
methodology.baseline: Fixed w_lr=0.01 (canonical at time of test)

content.root_cause: Per-step gradient signal is ~1e-8 magnitude. Would need ~1M steps for meaningful w_lr movement. The signal accumulation rate is 4-5 orders of magnitude too slow for the sequence lengths in the protocol (~1000 steps total).
content.note: This test also compared against canonical 0.01 rather than best fixed 0.0003. Even if signal were stronger, gradient accumulation faces the per-step vs sequence-level timescale gap.
